mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail and food services sales (RSAFS) exceed $350 billion, it indicates robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings growth, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the binary signal (RSAFS > 350000) versus forward 20-day returns is not consistently positive (rolling 2-year median IC < 0.02) or the hit rate does not exceed 55% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 8%, it signals substantial labor market slack that depresses aggregate demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as uncertainty spikes.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE > 8, the average forward 5-day return is not negative or the proportion of negative returns does not exceed 55% as assessed by a binomial test at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and CPI inflation year-over-year exceeds 2%, real interest rates are deeply negative, encouraging borrowing and risk-taking in equities, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with positive inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions are true, the average forward 5-day return is not positive, or the correlation between the joint signal and returns is non-positive over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE > 8%), a decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and confidence, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Within the regime UNRATE > 8, the Spearman rank correlation between the month-over-month change in UNRATE (negative is improvement) and forward 60-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over the available data.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,000 thousand units (annualized), it signals a strong construction sector that stimulates durable goods demand and employment, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1000, the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns is not greater than 55% (binomial test p > 0.05) or the hypothesis fails to outperform a long-only baseline over a full cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP increases sequentially (GDPC1 qoq growth) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) rises by more than 100 thousand in the same month, the joint expansion confirms broad economic strength, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce a positive average forward 20-day return, or the incremental improvement over individual signals (GDPC1 alone, PAYEMS alone) is not positive as measured by conditional Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is above its trailing 12-month average, it indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilisation, which supports corporate earnings and forecasts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The binary signal (INDPRO > 12m average) has a hit rate for positive 20-day returns below 52% over any 5-year rolling window, or the mean return advantage over unconditional market return is less than 50 basis points annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2% and the unemployment rate exceeds 6%, accommodative monetary policy amidst labor market slack delays wage-push inflation, sustaining low discount rates and positive equity valuations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields an average forward 20-day return that is not positive, or the excess return relative to periods outside the condition is negative over a full business cycle.
